sensi.name: galaxy-cover
sensi.alt: Sensi's relationship galaxy at rest: six senses as glowing constellations, every thread a coupling between two scores

sensi.name: demo-onboarding
sensi.alt: Onboarding demo: the copilot interviews one person and reveals the persona it will design for, comfort with a subject

sensi.name: onboarding
sensi.alt: Sensi's onboarding chat calibrating the comfort copilot to one person: their noise tolerance, their thermal grudges

sensi.name: app-shape
sensi.alt: The Sensi workspace mid-edit: a floor plan scored across six senses, conflicts flagged while the layout is still soft

sensi.name: green-lens
sensi.alt: The green lens over a city apartment plan: what shapes nature here, the kitchen flagged thirsty for daylight and plants

sensi.name: demo-report
sensi.alt: The vision report scrolling: room scores, the prompt they become, and the render the copilot hands back

sensi.name: report
sensi.alt: The vision report Sensi hands over: comfort scores shaped for one person, with the edits the layout absorbed

sensi.name: relationship-galaxy
sensi.alt: Animated relationship galaxy in Sensi, the comfort copilot, mapping how six sense scores pull on each other across a floor plan

sensi.name: sensory-layer
sensi.alt: The missing layer drawn onto the BIM stack: six senses above energy, cost and structure, the claim Sensi is built on

sensi.name: coupling-map
sensi.alt: The levers-to-senses coupling map, every link grounded in research from BS 8233 to ASHRAE before it entered the model

sensi.name: act-2-flow
sensi.alt: Act 2 of the pipeline as a flowchart: score, edit, ripple and galaxy loops around the plan while it is still soft

neurospace.name: demo-cover
neurospace.alt: The red parametric room mid-morph as a slider drags, the NeuroScore answering live in the corner of the lab

neurospace.name: landing
neurospace.alt: NeuroSpace landing page: enter the lab where sliders reshape a room and a live score estimates what it does to you

neurospace.name: view
neurospace.alt: The NeuroSpace lab in the browser: sliders reshape a parametric room while the live score answers back instantly

neurospace.name: pipeline
neurospace.alt: Pipeline diagram: slider values ride to Rhino.Compute for geometry while the browser scores the behavior instantly on its own

neurospace.name: score-formula
neurospace.alt: The NeuroScore formula opened up: five weighted dimensions and every assumption behind the estimate, readable and arguable

neurospace.name: score-1
neurospace.alt: Top of the NeuroSpace report: what this design predicts, hypothesis cards and the estimated wellbeing score dial

neurospace.name: score-2
neurospace.alt: The report's breakdown: contribution bars and a brain impact map behind the room's estimated wellbeing score

huddle.name: wasp-growth
huddle.alt: Animated WASP growth study: modules aggregating along wind and program across the Punta Arenas plot

huddle.name: axonometric
huddle.alt: Axonometric of The Huddle, wind-formed modules aggregated into a research hub inside the Punta Arenas grid

huddle.name: perspective-1
huddle.alt: Blizzard courtyard render inside The Huddle, turbines spinning between the wind-formed modules in Punta Arenas

huddle.name: climate-wind
huddle.alt: Climate analysis for Punta Arenas with monthly wind roses and the westerly wind speeds that drive the aggregation

huddle.name: global-index
huddle.alt: Global Index analysis matching Shield, Lens and Gill panels to daylight, wind and program across the envelope

huddle.name: sectioned-model
huddle.alt: Sectioned aggregation model showing public and private modules under the continuous wind-formed skin

huddle.name: perspective-2
huddle.alt: Street-level render of the module cluster meeting the town, the Punta Arenas sign in the foreground

huddle.name: axonometric-zoom
huddle.alt: Close-up of the aggregation at the gate, the three-panel envelope reading as a map of wind and light

lungs.name: demo-cover
lungs.alt: The analysis tower in the live viewer, program gradients in red and blue as the timeline scrubs across the studio platform

lungs.name: kpi-dashboard
lungs.alt: The KPI dashboard mid-studio: nine indicators scored live against their targets, honest oranges included

lungs.name: kpi-map
lungs.alt: The KPI dependency network: data, program and structure indicators tied into one system the studio steered by

lungs.name: speckle-live
lungs.alt: Speckle streams the design teams' tower model into the app, so performance gradients sit on the geometry itself

lungs.name: timeline
lungs.alt: Ten weeks as a live timeline: three team tracks and the weekly deliverables the app kept honest

lungs.name: stress-test
lungs.alt: The stress test tab, a calmness leaderboard the studio actually played; fewer pops means calmer

lungs.name: discover
lungs.alt: The Discover tab publishing the tower's vitals: three systems, one tower, and the numbers the studio stood behind

lungs.name: why-how-what
lungs.alt: The concept strip on the public page: the core problem, the data logic and the architecture in three beats

legoarch.name: demo-cover
legoarch.alt: Saint Basil's Cathedral solving into a brick layout in lEgoarCh, the stage with no AI in it on purpose

legoarch.name: pipeline
legoarch.alt: The lEgoarCh GenAI pipeline: FLUX and TRELLIS propose, deterministic code verifies the LEGO set as digitally buildable

legoarch.name: three-graphs
legoarch.alt: Three ComfyUI graphs share one core: the render, the LoRA look and the 3D mesh stages of the lEgoarCh pipeline

legoarch.name: lora-sweep
legoarch.alt: LoRA strength sweep from 0 to 1 on the Sagrada Familia and La Muralla Roja: the LEGO set look snaps in at full strength

legoarch.name: split-and-merge
legoarch.alt: The split and merge pass packing the voxel grid into the largest legal bricks, fewer parts holding the same shape

legoarch.name: perceptual-colour
legoarch.alt: Colour matching by CIEDE2000 instead of RGB distance, snapping every voxel to the nearest of 48 real LEGO colours

legoarch.name: mesh-vs-lego
legoarch.alt: The result page comparing generated mesh and placed bricks for the Sagrada Familia set, verified digitally buildable

legoarch.name: honest-part
legoarch.alt: The instructive failure: the Guggenheim prompt returned a voxel blob, structurally sound but not legible as architecture

legoarch.name: outputs
legoarch.alt: Everything one prompt returns: render, mesh, digitally verified build, box art, booklet, parts list and price

legoarch.name: real-parts
legoarch.alt: Real parts and real moulds: 44 BrickLink parts in 48 colours, the catalog the optimizer is allowed to build from

legoarch.name: shelf
legoarch.alt: The collection shelf holding three boxed sets the pipeline produced: Sagrada, La Muralla Roja and the Guggenheim run

cappelletti.name: galapagos-run
cappelletti.alt: Galapagos optimization running live, vertex octa lattice displacement traded against mass for the pavilion's outer shell

cappelletti.name: poster
cappelletti.alt: Cappelletti Pavilion render: the pasta-derived lattice shell at human scale beside a pond, visitors underneath

cappelletti.name: pasta-atlas
cappelletti.alt: The team's pasta atlas page: parametric equations of a cappelletti, the starting geometry for the pavilion's optimization

cappelletti.name: equations-script
cappelletti.alt: Grasshopper and Python script translating cappelletti equations into the pavilion shell ahead of structural optimization

cappelletti.name: optimization-map
cappelletti.alt: Workflow map of the structural optimization loop: Galapagos genomes, Alpaca4D analysis, mass and deflection fitness

cappelletti.name: lattice
cappelletti.alt: Edge octa lattice displacement study inside the shell, the inner structure that landed at 87 kg of material

cappelletti.name: two-topologies
cappelletti.alt: Structural optimization result: two lattice topologies, edge octa at 87 kg and vertex octa at 73 kg, both standing

ballooning-market.name: process

ballooning-market.alt: Aerial render of the Bab al-Luq block, the settled balloon roof filling the historic steel frame with color

ballooning-market.name: exploded-layers
ballooning-market.alt: Exploded layer diagram of the pneumatic occupation: historic steel frame, spine tunnel, and the inflated balloon roof

ballooning-market.name: algorithm
ballooning-market.alt: The Grasshopper algorithm behind the inflation simulation, from constraints to Kangaroo goals to the settled cluster

ballooning-market.name: epic-fails
ballooning-market.alt: The honest outtakes: zero self-collision and mesh clipping, the failures on the way to a settled balloon roof

ballooning-market.alt: Design-space matrix of Kangaroo inflation runs, tuning balloon scale, density and volume inside the frame

ballooning-market.name: render-3

narkomfin.name: graph-cover
narkomfin.alt: The Narkomfin voxel graph drawing closer: translucent volumes on black with graph nodes reaching out of the massing

narkomfin.name: voxel-graph
narkomfin.alt: The Narkomfin building rebuilt as translucent voxel volumes on black, spatial graph nodes and edges reaching out of the massing

narkomfin.alt: Simplified Narkomfin massing exploded level by level, the geometry the team sampled into spatial graphs

narkomfin.alt: Type F duplex levels of Narkomfin stacked as sampled grids, corridor connections running between the floors

narkomfin.name: betweenness
narkomfin.alt: Betweenness centrality over the team's spatial graph of Type K, one hot line showing every Narkomfin path crossing the corridor

narkomfin.name: communities-f
narkomfin.alt: Community detection across the three Type F levels, the spatial graph reading Ginzburg's duplexes as full-height vertical slices

narkomfin.name: graphsage-f
narkomfin.alt: The team's GraphSAGE room classifier reading conventional Type F rooms at 91.3 percent, graph machine learning as a plan reader

narkomfin.name: graphsage-k
narkomfin.alt: GraphSAGE prediction for the communal Type K unit at 67.9 percent, the team's model flagging the rooms that break domestic rules

urban-risk.name: assessment-ui
urban-risk.alt: The team's street safety assessment interface scoring a neighborhood's segments from OpenStreetMap features

urban-risk.name: safety-theories
urban-risk.alt: Six urban safety theories, Jacobs to Space Syntax, that the team encoded into measurable spatial features

urban-risk.name: london-data
urban-risk.alt: The London training data: 921,753 police incidents mapped citywide and broken down by crime type

urban-risk.name: pipeline
urban-risk.alt: The team's pipeline from OpenStreetMap data fetching through feature checks and risk scoring to model training and deployment

urban-risk.name: risk-score
urban-risk.alt: The composite risk score weighting lighting, visibility, connectivity, enclosure and transit per street segment

urban-risk.name: model-performance
urban-risk.alt: Three regression models flatlining on crime prediction, the run that showed spatial form alone predicts crime poorly

urban-risk.name: shap-explanations
urban-risk.alt: SHAP plots the team used to explain every prediction, comparing what drives risk in Islington and Barcelona's Eixample

urban-risk.name: spanish-neighborhoods
urban-risk.alt: The trained model scoring street segments in Poble Sec, Puente de Vallecas, and Triana, testing how the pipeline transfers

data-geometry.alt: Thermal comfort KPIs coloring the Hyperbuilding towers level by level, the team's Revit families carrying analysis into BIM

data-geometry.alt: Air purification KPIs as pollutant spheres along the cores, sized from Speckle data through Grasshopper and Rhino.Inside Revit

tsukiji.alt: A rainy visit to the Tsukiji outer market, the crowds and food stalls the redevelopment has to live beside

tsukiji.name: site-model
tsukiji.alt: The Tsukiji development brief and the Rhino site model, the venue massed at 74 m among Chuo City towers

tsukiji.name: climate-stress
tsukiji.alt: UTCI thermal stress maps from the team's environmental analysis: Tokyo swings the site from cold stress to summer heat

tsukiji.name: radiation-sun-hours

tsukiji.name: form-evolution
tsukiji.alt: Five form iterations from the team's environmental analysis, each revision answering a radiation, comfort or daylight study

podcast.name: demo-cover
podcast.alt: The podcast quote reel: seven timestamped lines from the conversation on architecture and the brain, in Emilie and Dr. Valentine's words

podcast.name: quote-icebreaker
podcast.alt: Quote card at 02:09, Emilie: we talk about smart buildings in terms of efficiency, but rarely in terms of feeling

podcast.name: quote-evidence
podcast.alt: Quote card at 09:21, Dr. Cleo Valentine: architecture has fallen into the trap of being artistically informed, not evidence informed

podcast.name: quote-naming
podcast.alt: Quote card at 09:21, Dr. Cleo Valentine blessing the term Behavior Information Modeling, an elegant way of describing the work

podcast.name: quote-gameengines
podcast.alt: Quote card at 42:53, Emilie: what if the building does not exist yet and we want to test it, where game engines come in

podcast.name: quote-publichealth
podcast.alt: Quote card at 55:59, Dr. Cleo Valentine: the built environment is one of the most underutilized tools for public health

podcast.name: quote-upset
podcast.alt: Quote card at 59:30, Dr. Cleo Valentine: people in unhealthy spaces are entitled to be upset because we can do better

podcast.name: quote-close
podcast.alt: Quote card at 1:01:54, Emilie closing: if spaces shape how you think and feel, maybe it is time we designed for that

podcast.name: episode-art
podcast.alt: Episode key art: a building cutaway splitting from grey efficiency into a warm, neuron-lit half, picturing the show's core question

homage.name: homage-cover

homage.alt: Moonlit render of the renovated Niemeyer housing bar at the Tripoli fair, a lit interior glowing over the reflecting deck

homage.name: strategy-quote

homage.alt: Exploded axonometric separating The Homage into past, present and future layers, paired with brain, lungs and heart icons

homage.alt: Night aerial of the Rachid Karami fairgrounds in Tripoli, the dark site with one beam of light from the intervention

astroidal.alt: The parametric equations of the astroidal ellipsoid written as Python, six coefficients driving the point grid

astroidal.name: board

chair-sim.alt: The settled chair rebuilt at growing voxel sizes, from fine grain steps to chunky rubber ring modules

chair-sim.alt: Eight voxelated chair iterations, each molded by a different sitting pose with damping and voxel size as the dials

chair-sim.name: gh-definition

playscape.alt: Section through the playscape: children climb the double net stretched over red inflatables above a padded ground

playscape.alt: Three playscape iterations comparing net rotation and elevated anchor settings above the inflatable mounds

playscape.name: net-drop
playscape.alt: The first climbing net falls onto the inflated mounds and tautens into a four point canopy in Kangaroo

verve.name: two-tower-elevation
verve.alt: Elevation of the twin Verve towers on their podium, 36 levels rising to 161 meters with planted balconies stepping up

verve.alt: Amenities level plan for the two Verve towers: gym, spa, yoga studio, cinema, kids pool and playgrounds on the podium

mars.alt: The full Rings of Mars competition board: exploded ring drawings, annotated plan, crater renders and systems diagram

mars.alt: Inside the ring: the farming and transport level, a researcher on the stair beside planted beds and the pod bay

xr.name: reaction-cover
xr.alt: An SN2 reaction rendered for AR: the chloride ion leaves the molecule as methanol forms, ball and stick in grey space

xr.alt: Board of three AR chemistry reactions, SN1, E2 and SN2, each with a molecular render and a view in AR QR code

xr.name: sn1-before
xr.alt: Frame from the SN1 AR lesson: the propanol molecule intact in ball and stick, the chloride ion still bonded

xr.name: sn1-after

encounter.name: encounter-cover
encounter.alt: The Encounter at a glance: the split bell tower, the tomb terraces from the air, the chapel light and the sunken court

encounter.name: bell-tower
encounter.alt: Looking up at the split bell tower of the Anfeh cemetery chapel, two concrete blades carrying the bells and the cross

encounter.name: cemetery-aerial
encounter.alt: Aerial of The Encounter in Anfeh, crescents of green roofed tombs circling a sunken court crossed by two long halls

encounter.name: sunken-court
encounter.alt: The sunken court at the heart of the cemetery, radial stairs climbing between planted tomb walls toward the light

encounter.name: chapel-light
encounter.alt: Inside the chapel, one thin roof slit washing light down board marked concrete onto a row of dark stone benches

encounter.name: moonlit-night
encounter.alt: Night view of the Anfeh cemetery, the split bell tower and curved hall above moonlit terraces of planted tombs

encounter.name: sunrise-spire
encounter.alt: The cemetery at sunrise, the cross topped spire rising behind the curved hall wall and planted tomb roofs

encounter.name: sketches-grid
encounter.alt: The first ink sketches of The Encounter: the folding axis, the hollow, the descent, the tomb walls and the gathering ring

encounter.name: floor-plan
encounter.alt: Labeled floor plan of The Encounter: bell tower, chapel, condolences hall and outdoor courtyard among the tomb crescents

encounter.name: section
encounter.alt: Long section through the cemetery, the spire and sunken steps between semi buried tombs and the tree lined slope

falcon.name: falcon-cover
falcon.alt: The falcon monument in motion: the camera pulls back from the copper calligraphy band to the whole roundabout at dusk

falcon.name: storm-takeoff-view
falcon.alt: Ground view under storm clouds, an airliner climbing overhead as the steel wings sweep up along the same take-off line

falcon.name: takeoff-angle-sketches
falcon.alt: Ink sketches on black of jets and a take-off diagram, tracing climb angles, flaps and slats that seed the monument's lines

falcon.name: aircraft-anatomy-sketches
falcon.alt: Ink sketches on black studying aircraft anatomy: control surfaces, flaps and spoilers, and the pitch, roll and yaw axes

falcon.name: process-01-mirroring
falcon.alt: Process stages 1 to 4: aircraft line drawing, take-off angles, then two mirrorings that fuse the lines into the falcon

falcon.name: process-02-lines-to-3d
falcon.alt: Process stages 5 to 8: the mirrored line drawings become a 3D body, connected through core, void, rotation and wings

falcon.name: process-03-landscape-merge
falcon.alt: Process stages 9 to 12: the roundabout landscape is redrawn from the same lines until sculpture and ground work as one

falcon.name: calligraphy-wings-exploded
falcon.alt: Exploded axonometric lifting the two pierced calligraphy plates in red off the white falcon wings, labeled in Arabic

falcon.name: site-plan
falcon.alt: Site plan of the falcon monument on the roundabout, mirrored wings on one axis with water channels and section lines

falcon.name: day-aerial-roundabout
falcon.alt: Daytime aerial of the full roundabout, paving and water channels radiating from the steel falcon like take-off lines

falcon.name: haze-sunset-prow
falcon.alt: The corten calligraphy prow against a hazy desert sunset, steel blades crossing above a carved landscape wall and berm

falcon.name: storm-elevation
falcon.alt: Side view of the falcon wings climbing against a storm sky, the pierced calligraphy band riding the leading edge

falcon.name: calligraphy-closeup-sunset
falcon.alt: Close-up of the pierced corten calligraphy at sunset, the low sun and gold water plaza seen through the desert haze

falcon.name: night-aerial
falcon.alt: Night aerial of the roundabout, the steel falcon lit at the center with water channels and headlights on the ring road

encounter-cert.name: certificate
encounter-cert.alt: The certificate of achievement from Ctrl Act Design naming Emilie Chidiac, shortlisted as finalist of the Cemetery Challenge
